entries.d: To do something to see if you like it or can do it
entries.s: I've never played tennis before, but I want to _____ it at the tennis club this Saturday morning.

entries.d: To stop trying to do something
entries.s: Learning the guitar is hard, but please don't _____. Keep practising every day and you will improve.

entries.d: A short performance to show you can sing, act or dance
entries.s: She sang a pop song at her _____ for the school musical.

entries.w: to have got what it takes
entries.d: To have the skills or talent that you need
entries.s: You are a great dancer and full of talent — you've _____ what it takes to win the competition!

entries.d: To succeed or to be able to come
entries.s: Sorry, I can't _____ it to your birthday party — I have an important dentist appointment at the same time.

entries.w: to be on the way up
entries.g: auf dem Weg nach oben sein
entries.d: To become more and more successful
entries.s: The young rock band is _____. They play bigger concerts every month and their songs are becoming popular.
mc: to be on the way out
to be on the way down

entries.d: To answer or contact someone later
entries.s: I'm busy right now, but I'll _____ back to you with an answer after lunch at 2 o'clock.

entries.d: To have the same idea or opinion as someone else
entries.s: I _____ with you completely — chocolate ice cream really is the best flavour of all ice cream in the world.

entries.d: To be the property of someone
entries.s: This blue jacket isn't mine — it _____ to my older brother Tom, so I have to give it back to him later.

entries.d: To do something special for a happy event
entries.s: On New Year's Eve, people _____ with colourful fireworks, dancing, and parties at midnight when the new year begins.

entries.d: Very, very much
entries.s: The water in the swimming pool was _____ cold — much colder than I expected and my lips turned blue!

entries.d: A musical instrument that you blow across
entries.s: She plays the _____ in the school orchestra — a long thin silver instrument you blow into.

entries.d: A person who sings, often as a job
entries.s: My favourite _____ has a new album out this week with ten new songs that she wrote and recorded.

entries.d: Having done well or achieved what you wanted
entries.s: The school play was very _____ — all 300 tickets were sold and the audience gave a standing ovation.

entries.d: Having a natural skill to do something well
entries.s: My little brother is very _____ at drawing — he never took any lessons but draws portraits like a professional artist.

entries.d: To make a liquid fall out of its container by mistake
entries.s: Be careful with that glass or you'll _____ your juice all over the clean white table and make a mess.

entries.d: All of something, complete
entries.s: She ate the _____ large pizza by herself in one sitting because she was so hungry after the match.

entries.d: A person who gives their opinion about music, films or books
entries.s: The film _____ in the newspaper wrote that the new Hollywood film was boring and too long.

entries.d: Not afraid, ready to do difficult or dangerous things
entries.s: The firefighter was very _____ and ran into the burning building to save the little girl on the top floor.

entries.d: Used to say something surprising — less than you expected
entries.s: The maths test was so easy but he got _____ one answer right — zero out of ten questions correct.

entries.d: A set of matching jacket and trousers for formal events
entries.s: My dad wears a dark grey business _____ with matching trousers, jacket, and tie when he goes to the office.

entries.d: Not happy, feeling sad
entries.s: She was very _____ and could not stop crying because her best friend had moved to another country.

entries.d: To use something badly or use more than you need
entries.s: Don't _____ good food by throwing it in the bin — eat everything on your plate. Think of hungry people.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with WASTE?

entries.d: To have a feeling or emotion inside you
entries.s: After the long day, I _____ very tired and just wanted to sleep.

entries.d: To not enjoy something any more because you did it too often
entries.s: I _____ tired of eating the same cheese sandwich every day and asked mum for something different in my lunchbox.
mc: to fall in love with sth.

entries.d: The words of a song
entries.s: I love this pop song, but I can never remember the _____ — the words that the singer sings in each verse.

entries.d: To create something new from your imagination
entries.s: My little sister likes to _____ funny imaginary stories about dragons and princesses before bedtime.

entries.d: A round, flat disc that plays music when you put it on a player
entries.s: My mum has a dusty box of old black vinyl _____ from the 1980s that she still plays on quiet Sunday afternoons.

entries.d: To look like or appear to be
entries.s: The chocolate cake _____ delicious when I saw it, but when I tasted it, it was actually very salty.

entries.d: To join in when a melody is playing
entries.s: Everyone in the car started to _____ loudly to their favourite pop song playing on the radio.

entries.d: A piece of music or the main melody of a song
entries.s: I know this familiar _____ very well but I can't remember the name of the song right now.

entries.d: A way to say you really don't like something
entries.s: The music coming from next door is so loud and terrible — I _____!

entries.d: A way to say something is OK for you
entries.s: We can watch that action film if you want to — I _____ at all, it's fine with me.

entries.d: To go somewhere with someone
entries.s: We're all going to the park to play football. Do you want to _____ with us?

entries.d: To happen at a certain time and location
entries.s: The Olympic Games _____ every four years in a different host country around the world, with thousands of athletes.

entries.d: After that, later
entries.s: We had lunch at noon in the park, and _____ we all went swimming at the pool at two o'clock.

entries.d: Except for, other than
entries.s: _____ the heavy rain in the afternoon, the whole trip to the zoo was really fun and we saw all the animals.

entries.d: A phrase to introduce what you think
entries.s: _____, the best film this year was the one about the lost dog who travels 1000 miles to find his family.

entries.d: To want to know more about something
entries.s: He is very _____ space and astronomy — he reads every book about planets he can find at the library.

entries.d: A way to agree when someone says they don't do or like something
entries.s: 'I don't like spiders at all.' — '_____ They scare me too.'
